redFlags.title: Not Disclosing Cash and Bank Balance in Schedule BP (NEW FY 2025-26)
redFlags.description: From FY 2025-26, Schedule BP in ITR-4 MANDATORILY requires disclosure of cash balance in hand and aggregate bank balance across all accounts as on March 31, 2026. Leaving these fields blank or entering zero when actual balance exists is a compliance violation.
redFlags.consequence: Tax department has access to AIS which includes bank balance data from financial institutions. Non-disclosure or underdisclosure triggers scrutiny notice u/s 143(2) and may invoke Section 69A (unexplained cash credit).
redFlags.howToDetect: Download AIS from income tax portal — it will show deposits in bank accounts. Cross-check with ITR disclosure.
redFlags.correction: Always verify March 31, 2026 closing balance of all bank accounts and physical cash before filing. Enter accurate figures.
redFlags.legalReference: Schedule BP, ITR-4 Form for AY 2026-27; Section 69A, Income-tax Act

redFlags.title: High Bank Balance Inconsistent with Declared Presumptive Income
redFlags.description: If a 44AD trader declares presumptive income of ₹5 lakh but has bank balance of ₹50 lakh as on March 31, 2026, the department will question the source of the large bank balance relative to declared income. This is a common pattern in unexplained wealth cases.
redFlags.consequence: Scrutiny assessment u/s 143(2). Potential addition of unexplained amount as income u/s 69.
redFlags.prevention: Maintain documentation: bank statements showing source of balance (prior year savings, loan drawdowns, inheritance, etc.); declare accurate income; if income is higher than 8%/50% minimum, consider declaring higher.

redFlags.title: Using ITR-4 When Capital Gains Exceed ₹1.25L or Are of Wrong Type
redFlags.description: For FY 2025-26, ITR-4 allows ONLY LTCG u/s 112A within ₹1.25L. Any of these situations require ITR-2/ITR-3: (a) LTCG u/s 112A > ₹1.25L, (b) Any STCG u/s 111A, (c) LTCG on property or unlisted shares, (d) Capital losses being carried forward.
redFlags.consequence: Return treated as defective. Possible under-reporting of capital gains tax.
redFlags.correction: Switch to ITR-2 (if no business income) or ITR-3 (if business income) when any disqualifying capital gain exists.

redFlags.title: Using ITR-4 with More Than 2 House Properties
redFlags.description: ITR-4 now allows up to 2 house properties (expanded from 1 for FY 2025-26). However, 3 or more properties still disqualify ITR-4.
redFlags.consequence: Defective return notice. Non-disclosure of additional property income.
redFlags.correction: Switch to ITR-3 if owning 3+ house properties.

redFlags.title: Declaring Income Below Minimum Presumptive Rate Without Books
redFlags.description: Under Section 44AD, minimum declared income must be 8% of turnover (6% for digital transactions). Under 44ADA, minimum is 50% of gross receipts. If actual profit is lower and taxpayer declares actual profit without maintaining books, this is invalid.
redFlags.consequence: The ITR is treated as non-compliant with presumptive scheme. Department can assess income at the minimum rate. Tax audit becomes mandatory for that year.
redFlags.correction: Either: (a) Declare at least the minimum presumptive rate, OR (b) Maintain regular books, get audit if applicable, and file ITR-3.

redFlags.title: Re-Entering Presumptive Scheme After Opting Out (5-Year Lock-In)
redFlags.description: Section 44AD(4) prohibits a taxpayer from using the 44AD scheme for 5 FYs after they once opted out (i.e., declared income below 8% or maintained books). This is frequently violated.
redFlags.consequence: Return treated as defective. Assessment at minimum presumptive rate. Penalties for concealment if income was underreported in intervening years.
redFlags.example: If taxpayer filed ITR-3 (books) in FY 2022-23 after having used 44AD in FY 2021-22, they cannot use 44AD again until FY 2027-28 (5 years: 2022-23, 2023-24, 2024-25, 2025-26, 2026-27).

redFlags.title: Turnover Exceeds 44AD/44ADA Limit
redFlags.description: 44AD: Turnover > ₹3 Crore (or ₹2 Crore if cash transactions > 5%). 44ADA: Gross receipts > ₹75 lakh (or ₹50 lakh if cash > 5%). These limits are calculated for the entire financial year.
redFlags.consequence: Cannot use ITR-4. Must file ITR-3 with books. If turnover > ₹1 Crore (44AD) or receipts > ₹50L (44ADA), tax audit u/s 44AB required.
redFlags.correction: Switch to ITR-3. Maintain books of accounts for the year.

redFlags.title: GST Turnover Does Not Match ITR Turnover
redFlags.description: For businesses registered under GST, the turnover reported in GSTR-1/GSTR-3B and GSTR-9 is compared with ITR turnover by the income tax department. Significant discrepancy triggers notices.
redFlags.consequence: Income tax notice for reconciliation. If ITR turnover is significantly lower, may be treated as unexplained income.
redFlags.prevention: Before filing, reconcile GST turnover (from GSTR-9) with ITR turnover. Small differences (credit notes, exempt supply, etc.) should be explainable.

redFlags.title: Not Paying Advance Tax on Large Presumptive Income
redFlags.description: Presumptive income taxpayers with estimated tax liability > ₹10,000 must pay advance tax. While 44AD/44ADA taxpayers can pay the entire amount in one installment by March 15, 2026, not paying by that date results in interest u/s 234B.
redFlags.consequence: Interest u/s 234B at 1% per month on unpaid advance tax from April 1, 2026 to date of assessment.
redFlags.correction: Calculate advance tax liability and pay by March 15, 2026 via Challan 280.
redFlags.legalReference: Sections 207-210, 234B, Income-tax Act; Proviso to Section 211(1)

redFlags.title: HUF Using 44ADA — Professional Income Not Available for HUF
redFlags.description: Section 44ADA is available ONLY to resident individual taxpayers. HUFs are not eligible for 44ADA. If a HUF files ITR-4 using 44ADA, the return is technically defective.
redFlags.consequence: Defective return. HUF must use 44AD (for business) or 44AE (for transport) to file ITR-4.
redFlags.correction: HUF with professional income must file ITR-3 with full books and possibly get audit done.
redFlags.legalReference: Section 44ADA, Income-tax Act — applicable to 'resident individual' only

redFlags.title: More Than 10 Vehicles Owned Under 44AE
redFlags.description: Section 44AE applies only if the taxpayer owns not more than 10 vehicles at any point during the financial year. If vehicles exceed 10 at any time, 44AE cannot be used for the entire year.
redFlags.consequence: Must use actual books of accounts and file ITR-3.

redFlags.title: Zero or Very Low Declared Profit Inconsistent with Lifestyle
redFlags.description: If a 44AD trader declares income of ₹1.5 lakh (minimum 8% of ₹18L turnover) but owns luxury assets, travels internationally, or has significant investments, AIS will show these purchases — creating an inconsistency with declared income.
redFlags.consequence: Risk-based scrutiny selection under Section 143(2). Burden of proof on taxpayer to explain.
redFlags.prevention: Declare income that reasonably reflects economic activity. If actual profit is higher than minimum, declare higher voluntarily to avoid scrutiny.

validationErrors.errorMessage: Cash and bank balance fields in Schedule BP are blank
validationErrors.cause: Mandatory fields left empty — not allowed from FY 2025-26
validationErrors.resolution: Fill in actual cash in hand and bank balance as on March 31, 2026

validationErrors.errorMessage: Declared income (%) less than minimum presumptive rate
validationErrors.cause: Declared income < 8% of turnover (44AD) or < 50% of receipts (44ADA)
validationErrors.resolution: Either increase declared income to minimum, or switch to ITR-3 with books

validationErrors.errorMessage: LTCG u/s 112A declared in ITR-4 exceeds ₹1,25,000
validationErrors.cause: Schedule CG shows taxable LTCG above the ITR-4 allowed limit

validationErrors.errorMessage: More than 2 house property entries found in Schedule HP
validationErrors.cause: Three or more properties listed — ITR-4 limit is 2 properties

validationErrors.errorMessage: Form 10-IEA not filed for old regime selection with business income
validationErrors.cause: Taxpayer selected old regime but has not filed Form 10-IEA
validationErrors.resolution: File Form 10-IEA before or simultaneously with ITR-4 filing
